title: Derive MODES array from DatabaseMode type using satisfies to prevent desync
description: DatabaseMode union type (DatabaseModule.tsx:75) and MODES array (:90) are the same data defined in two places. Adding a mode to one but not the other causes no TypeScript error. Fix: use 'as const satisfies' pattern so TypeScript enforces the array matches the type. Additionally, add exhaustive switch in the canvas memo (:4079) by removing the default case — TypeScript will then error if a new mode is added to the type without handling in the switch.
acceptanceCriteria: MODES array uses 'as const satisfies readonly { id: DatabaseMode; ... }[]'
Adding a new mode to DatabaseMode type without adding to MODES produces TypeScript error
Canvas switch at :4079 has no default case — uses exhaustive check (never type)
Adding a mode to DatabaseMode without handling in switch produces TypeScript error

title: Replace module-level mutable ID counters with instance-scoped counters
description: btree-viz.ts:27 (_btreeIdCounter), lsm-viz.ts:38 (_lsmIdCounter), query-plan.ts:32 (_planIdCounter) are module-level let variables that persist across all class instances and never reset (except _planIdCounter). This breaks isolation between instances and could cause ID collisions. Move counters inside the class as private instance fields. Also fix sample-er-diagrams.ts:22 (_sampleIdCounter) which uses resetIds() that causes cross-sample ID overlap.
acceptanceCriteria: BTreeViz has private idCounter instance field instead of module-level let
LSMTreeViz has private idCounter instance field
QueryPlanNode ID generation is instance-scoped
Sample ER diagram IDs use UUIDs or non-overlapping ranges instead of shared counter
Creating two BTreeViz instances produces non-conflicting IDs

title: Unify NormalizationResult type — remove duplicate definition in DatabaseModule
description: NormalizationResult at DatabaseModule.tsx:483 duplicates fields from NormalizationState at types.ts:59. Two types for the same concept in different files. Unify: extend NormalizationState in types.ts with the additional result fields (closure, decomposition) OR define NormalizationResult in types.ts and import it in DatabaseModule. Single source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Only ONE normalization result type exists (in types.ts or a dedicated result-types.ts)
DatabaseModule.tsx imports the type instead of defining its own
No duplicate interface definitions for the same concept

title: Narrow ERAttribute.type from string to union of known SQL types with string fallback
description: ERAttribute.type (types.ts:15) is bare 'string' — accepts anything. The TYPE_MAP in er-to-sql.ts:17-39 lists 20 known SQL types (INT, VARCHAR, TEXT, BOOLEAN, etc.). Narrow the type to a union of these known types plus string fallback for custom types. This enables autocomplete in the ER builder and catches typos at the type level.
acceptanceCriteria: ERAttribute.type changed from 'string' to 'SQLType | string' where SQLType is a union of known types
SQLType union includes all 20 types from TYPE_MAP
ER diagram builder input gets autocomplete suggestions for known types
Custom types still accepted via string fallback

title: Separate EREntity domain data from layout position (x, y)
description: EREntity at types.ts:28 mixes domain data (name, attributes, isWeak) with layout position (x, y). This violates separation of concerns — canvas layout should be managed separately from entity data. Split into EREntityData (domain) and EREntityPosition (layout). Benefits: (1) entity data can be serialized without layout, (2) layout can change without touching domain types, (3) enables auto-layout algorithms that only modify positions.
acceptanceCriteria: EREntityData interface contains: id, name, isWeak, attributes (no x, y)
EREntityPosition interface contains: entityId, x, y
Canvas manages a separate Map<string, EREntityPosition> for layout
ER entity serialization (for SQL generation, sample loading) doesn't include x, y
Existing drag behavior still works via position map

title: Remove unused TransactionOp type from types.ts
description: TransactionOp (types.ts:75) is defined but NEVER used anywhere in the codebase. The actual type used for transaction steps is TransactionStep (transaction-sim.ts:12). TransactionOp was likely an early design that was superseded. Dead types confuse developers and add maintenance burden. Remove it.
acceptanceCriteria: TransactionOp interface removed from types.ts
No import of TransactionOp exists anywhere in the codebase
index.ts barrel export updated (if it was exported)
All tests pass after removal
